A squirrel is sitting in front of the second hollow. It says: "There are no nuts in the other hollow." Since red squirrels always tell the truth, this statement implies that the other hollow (the first one) has no nuts. If the squirrel is red, then the other hollow has no nuts.

SECTION 2  
The squirrel also says: "There is at least one nut in one of the hollows." This statement is true because if the other hollow has no nuts, then the first hollow must have at least one nut.
